en.name: Binbin Lin
en.title:
Associate Professor
(Tianyou Distinguished Track)
en.affiliation:
Faculty of Geomatics
Lanzhou Jiaotong University
en.bio:
Dr. Binbin Lin is an Associate Professor (Tianyou Distinguished Track) in the Faculty of Geomatics at Lanzhou Jiaotong University. She received her Ph.D. in Geography from Texas A&M University in 2025 under the supervision of Prof. Lei Zou.
Her research lies at the intersection of GIScience, Geospatial Artificial Intelligence (GeoAI), social sensing, geospatial big data, public health, and spatiotemporal analytics, with a particular focus on understanding human-environment interactions and developing data-driven approaches to address challenges in public health, disasters, and sustainable cities.

zh.name: 林彬彬
zh.title: 讲师（校聘天佑副教授）
zh.affiliation: 兰州交通大学 测绘与地理信息学院
zh.address: 兰州交通大学笃行楼B座1502
zh.bio:
林彬彬，兰州交通大学测绘与地理信息学院天佑副教授，博士毕业于美国德州农工大学（Texas A&M University）地理学专业，师从邹雷教授。
主要从事地理信息科学（GIScience）、地理人工智能（GeoAI）、社会感知、地理空间大数据、公共健康地理及时空分析与建模等方向研究，致力于融合多源地理空间数据、人工智能与空间分析方法，揭示人类行为、健康、灾害与城市系统之间的时空耦合关系，为公共健康治理、灾害韧性提升和可持续城市建设提供数据驱动的空间智能解决方案。

en.title: Profile
en.body: Dr. Binbin Lin is an Associate Professor (Tianyou Distinguished Track) in the Faculty of Geomatics at Lanzhou Jiaotong University. She received her Ph.D. in Geography from Texas A&M University in 2025 under the supervision of Prof. Lei Zou. Her research lies at the intersection of GIScience, Geospatial Artificial Intelligence (GeoAI), social sensing, geospatial big data, public health, and spatiotemporal analytics, with a particular focus on understanding human-environment interactions and developing data-driven approaches to address challenges in public health, disasters, and sustainable cities.

zh.title: 教师简介
zh.body: 林彬彬，兰州交通大学测绘与地理信息学院天佑副教授，博士毕业于美国德州农工大学（Texas A&M University）地理学专业，师从邹雷教授。主要从事地理信息科学（GIScience）、地理人工智能（GeoAI）、社会感知、地理空间大数据、公共健康地理及时空分析与建模等方向研究，致力于融合多源地理空间数据、人工智能与空间分析方法，揭示人类行为、健康、灾害与城市系统之间的时空耦合关系，为公共健康治理、灾害韧性提升和可持续城市建设提供数据驱动的空间智能解决方案。

en.title: Geospatial Big Data and GeoAI
en.text: Geospatial artificial intelligence, spatial machine learning, spatiotemporal modeling, and spatial decision support.

zh.title: 地理大数据与地理人工智能
zh.text: 围绕地理空间大数据组织、时空建模、GeoAI 方法与空间决策支持开展研究。

en.title: Public Health Geography
en.text: Healthcare accessibility, health inequality, mental health, occupational stress, and health system disruption.

zh.title: 公共健康与健康地理
zh.text: 关注医疗可达性、公共健康不平等、心理健康、职业压力以及扰动情境下的健康系统响应。

en.title: Social Sensing and Human Mobility
en.text: Social media, mobile phone location data, public sentiment, behavioral signals, and crisis response.

zh.title: 社会感知与人群流动
zh.text: 利用社交媒体、手机位置数据与行为感知数据，刻画公众情绪、出行行为与危机响应。

en.title: Disaster and Urban Resilience
en.text: Spatial exposure, vulnerability, recovery, adaptation, and resilience of cities and health systems.

zh.title: 灾害影响与城市韧性
zh.text: 研究灾害扰动下城市与健康系统的空间暴露、脆弱性、恢复、适应与韧性。

items: 2024–2025. Research Assistant. National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine (NASEM) Gulf Research Program (GRP). "Climate-LEAD: Climate Effects on Localized Environmental Health Disparities in Overburdened Texas Communities along the Gulf Coast." PI: Lei Zou, Texas A&M University. (US$1,499,990).

2023–2025. Research Assistant. National Science Foundation (NSF) Human Networks and Data Science (HNDS). "Collaborative Research: HNDS-I: A Cyberinfrastructure for Multiscale Human Dynamics and Resilience (HDR) Research." PI: Nina Lam, Louisiana State University. (US$1,096,902).

2022–2023. Research Assistant. Texas A&M Seed Funds for Collaboration among Three Merging Colleges. "Statistical Machine Learning Meets High-Dimensional Spatiotemporal Challenges: A Case Study of COVID-19 Modeling and Prediction." PI: Ning Ning, Texas A&M University.

2021–2023. Research Assistant. Texas A&M Institute of Data Sciences – Data Resource Development Program. "Tracking Social and Governmental Responses to COVID-19 Using Geospatial Big Data." PI: Lei Zou, Texas A&M University. (US$27,000).

2021–2023. Research Assistant. Texas A&M Innovation [X] Program. "Restoring Happiness: Leveraging GeoAI and Social Engagement to Address Happiness Inequalities after COVID-19 and Winter Storm Uri." PI: Heng Cai, Texas A&M University. (US$20,000).

groups.title: 参与项目
items: 2024–2025，研究助理，美国国家科学院、工程院与医学院（NASEM）墨西哥湾研究计划（GRP），《Climate-LEAD：气候对墨西哥湾沿岸德州重负社区局部环境健康差异的影响》，负责人：邹雷（Texas A&M University）。
2023–2025，研究助理，美国国家科学基金会（NSF）人类网络与数据科学（HNDS）项目，《协作研究：HNDS-I：多尺度人类动态与韧性（HDR）研究的网络基础设施》，负责人：Nina Lam（Louisiana State University）。
2022–2023，研究助理，德州农工大学三院联合合作种子基金，《统计机器学习应对高维时空挑战——以 COVID-19 建模与预测为案例研究》，负责人：Ning Ning（Texas A&M University）。
2021–2023，研究助理，德州农工大学数据科学研究所-数据资源开发项目，《利用地理空间大数据追踪 COVID-19 的社会和政府响应》，负责人：邹雷（Texas A&M University）。
2021–2023，研究助理，德州农工大学创新[X]项目，《恢复幸福感：利用 GeoAI 与社会参与应对 COVID-19 和冬季风暴 Uri 后幸福感不平等》，负责人：蔡衡（Texas A&M University）。

en.body: Prospective students interested in GIScience, GeoAI, public health geography, social sensing, spatial data science, and disaster resilience are welcome to contact me.
